I believe these actions could lead to a potential re-rating of Teladoc's valuation, however, this depends on management's ability to execute and reduce costs, improve capital allocation, or to pursue strategic separation. This could improve investor perception and lead to higher multiples that are closer to peers.

Valuation and Improving Financials

Teladoc has historically had trouble with profitability, even with recent improvements. One of these issues is BetterHelp, one of the companies largest revenue contributors, which has generated lower margins because of high customer acquisition costs and high customer turnover. This forces Teladoc to continue investing in marketing to support BetterHelp, which limits profits even when the business is growing.

On the other hand, Teladoc's integrated care segment has stronger margins as a B2B model, which has helped offset the drag in BetterHelp's D2C model. However, Teladoc is making steps to reduce this drag by growing its insurance-reimbursement based models and AI-driven systems. Although the company still faces some of this drag, the transition seems to be taking shape as results continue to improve from previous years.

Teladoc generated $167 million in FCF in 2025, marking a strong turnaround from negative levels in prior years. It used this money to pay off its outstanding convertible notes and to lower its debt. Due to the lower near-term debt levels, Teladoc now has approximately $781 million in cash, and its short-term debt obligations have been significantly lowered.

For 2026, Teladoc is expecting revenue of $2.47-2.59 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $266-308 million. This would represent an approximate 11% margin on revenue at the mid-point. While modest, the progression of EBITDA from negative three years ago to over $200M today demonstrates the business' earnings potential. Management expects that BetterHelp will generate adjusted EBITDA margins of 3-4.6% as they continue to invest in it, which is similar to the 4.4% adjusted EBITDA margin in 2025.

The difference in the valuation multiples of Teladoc relative to its peer group is relatively large. With pure D2C peers like Hims & Hers trading at ~1.7x forward revenue and pure B2B Amwell (which has plummeting sales) trading at ~0.46x, Teladoc's multiples appear unfairly low. Based on a ~0.38x forward revenue multiple, the market cap at approximately $940 million represents less than 5x adjusted EBITDA based on current operations. Additionally, the market should assign some credit to increased revenue and higher margins associated with the growth of BetterHelp's insurance revenue base and Pulse's margin contributions. The situation has continued to improve for Teladoc, yet the multiples are still depressed. This ultimately gives a favorable risk/reward at current levels and as a result, I'm rating Teladoc a Buy.

Risks

The most significant and uncertain assumptions are BetterHelp's increased revenue from its insurance business. Expansion into all 50 states will require solid execution and if any problems are incurred it could hurt management's revenue guidance for the segment.

There continues to be a high level of competitive activity in D2C mental health services with companies such as Cerebral and Talkspace (TALK), which continue to advertise heavily for their services. A rapid decline in D2C base customers due to competition could lead guidance misses.

Conclusion

Teladoc Health is now positioned as a next-gen healthcare platform, not just a traditional telehealth provider. With the transition to an insurance-based revenue model, and the increased scope of its B2B integrated care segment, Teladoc is creating a foundation for predictability of revenue growth and sustainable margin improvement. Overall, this leaves Teladoc with a structurally stronger business than what is reflected in the price of the stock.

Another key factor for the new Teladoc is PulseAI, which expands the value proposition of the company. Through the use of Teladoc's large data set to proactively identify and manage high-risk patients, Teladoc is moving along the healthcare value chain (from access to care to actual improved health outcomes and reduced costs). These improvements enhance client retention, support premium pricing, and capture long-term contracts.

In terms of the financial aspects of the company, it is shifting into a new phase of operational leverage. Teladoc has positive FCF, a strengthened balance sheet, and increasing EBITDA margins which indicates that the company is transitioning to an efficient and scalable profitability model. The combination of this, along with the undervaluation of the company when compared to peers, creates an opportunity where even modest execution could drive a substantial increase in valuation. As a result of the growing fundamentals of Teladoc's business, I believe the company could see a re-rating over time if management continues to successfully execute, which is why I am rating Teladoc a Buy at current levels.
